content.full_text:
{b'\\1}"'
-""N-,
MICHAEL B. BIXLER
1192 Seacoast Drive
Imperial Beach, CA 91932
Apil23, 1992
Fair Political Practices Commission
Irgal Department
428 "J" Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814
Dear Sirs:
An issue has been raised as to a potential conflict of interest I may have with regard to an
upcoming vote on a local coastal program amendment which would have the effect of
creating a limited-time/permit parking plan for Elm, Evergreen, and Elder Avenues, from
Seacoast Drive east to Second, and for both sides of Second between Elm and Elder, in the
City of Imperial Beach.
In essence, the parking plan would allow residents and their guests a means to park beyond
the posted two hour limit. At the present time, there is no parking limitation. Enclosed are
the staff report on the item, the public hearing notice and map of the area.
I am the sole beneficiary of a trust that is the record owner of a duplex which is located on
206 Elderl957 Second Street, which is in the proposed parking plan area. The lot area is
approximately 3,200 sq. ft. and I estimate its fair market value as $250,000.
My home is located on a double lot which consists of my home (1192,{ Seacoast Drive) and
another home which is rented (11928 Seacoast Drive). The total area of the two lots is
approximately 12,000 sq. ft. and I estimate the fair market value to be $1,250,000. This
property is located approximately 1,331 feet from the parking plan area.
Thank you for your consideration in this matter.
Sincerely,
/rtlt/"""A/i4
Michael B. Bixler
Mayor, City of Imperial Beach
Enclosures
MBB:lb

sections.question: An issue has been raised as to a potential conflict of interest I may have with regard to an upcoming vote on a local coastal program amendment which would have the effect of creating a limited-time/permit parking plan for Elm, Evergreen, and Elder Avenues, from Seacoast Drive east to Second, and for both sides of Second between Elm and Elder, in the City of Imperial Beach.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the requester, as Mayor of Imperial Beach, have a conflict of interest in voting on a proposed parking plan that would affect properties in which he has a financial interest?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a public official seeking FPPC advice, not an FPPC response. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are explicitly labeled. The question is synthesized from the opening paragraph where the conflict of interest issue is described. Document contains OCR artifacts and formatting irregularities but core information is extractable.

embedding.summary: Michael B. Bixler, Mayor of Imperial Beach, requests FPPC advice regarding a potential conflict of interest related to an upcoming vote on a local coastal program amendment establishing a limited-time permit parking plan in specific areas of the city. Bixler discloses that he is the sole beneficiary of a trust owning a duplex within the affected parking plan area and also owns residential property nearby.
